{{Infobox character
| name = Kelsa
| aliases = Wei Shi Kelsa
| appears_in = Cradle
| titles = Copper Ruler, Wei Shi, Truegold
| abilities = Path of the White Fox, Fox Dream Ruler technique, White Fox madra, Foxfire, Foxtail Enforcer technique, Striker technique, Fox Dream
| affiliations = Wei clan, Twin Star sect
| introduced = cradle-01-unsouled
}}

''See also: [[Kelsa Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Kelsa, also known as Wei Shi Kelsa, is a tall, athletic [[sacred artist]] of the [[Wei clan]] and the sister of [[Lindon]]. A practitioner on the [[Path of the White Fox]], she utilizes light and dream-based [[madra]] to create illusions, such as her [[Fox Dream]] technique or the [[Fox Mirror]], and can manifest foxfire in shades of purple and white. She is noted for her intense focus, discipline, and direct personality. Kelsa has demonstrated significant talent and rapid progression throughout her training. She reached the threshold of the [[Iron stage]] at sixteen, an age far earlier than most [[sacred artists]] in her clan. Following her advancement to [[Iron]], she became the highest-ranking young [[Iron]] in the [[Wei clan]]. Her progression continued through the stages of [[Jade]], [[Lowgold]], [[Truegold]], and eventually [[Highgold]]. During her development, she worked to establish a stable [[Skyhunter Iron body]]. Throughout various conflicts involving the [[Wei clan]], the [[Kazan]], and the [[Heaven's Glory]] School, Kelsa has served as a combatant and a leader. She has participated in battles involving swordplay, spears, and foxfire, and has been tasked with roles such as managing a training hall. In later developments, she has acted as a spokesperson for her brother, the [[Void Sage]], and has worked alongside allies like [[Orthos]] to protect her family and navigate the dangers of the [[Blackflame Empire]].}}

== Unsouled ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Kelsa is the sister of [[Lindon]] and a [[sacred artist]] following the [[Path of the White Fox]], specializing in the aspects of light and dreams. As a [[Copper]] [[Ruler]] wearing orange shadesilk training clothes and a scepter-marked badge, she reached the barrier to the [[Iron stage]] at sixteen years old. After advancing to the [[Iron stage]], she became the highest-ranking young [[Iron]] within the [[Wei clan]] and sat beside [[Lindon]] in the stands to watch his [[arena]] match with horror. During this time, she warned [[Lindon]] not to overexert himself and vowed to retaliate against anyone who shamed him during his trials. Kelsa also appeared in [[Lindon]]'s visions, where she was seen kneeling and weeping in a dream before eventually receiving a [[jade]] badge from [[Patriarch Sairus]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] is [[Lindon]]'s sister.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2">''Unsouled'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] is a [[Copper]] [[Ruler]] who wears orange shadesilk training clothes and a copper badge marked with a scepter.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c5">''Unsouled'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] is a [[sacred artist]] on the [[Path of the White Fox]] who cultivates aspects of dreams and light.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] reached the barrier between [[Copper]] and [[Iron]] at the age of sixteen.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] is on the verge of condensing [[Iron]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] is a [[Copper stage]] practitioner.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6">''Unsouled'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] advanced to the [[Iron stage]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c7">''Unsouled'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] tells [[Lindon]] not to push himself too hard and threatens to pay back those who shame him in the [[Iron]] trials.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c9">''Unsouled'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] is kneeling and crying in [[Lindon]]'s dream.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c10">''Unsouled'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] is a person seen in [[Lindon]]'s visions who eventually receives a [[jade]] badge from [[Patriarch Sairus]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11">''Unsouled'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] sat next to [[Lindon]] in the stands.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] watched the [[arena]] match with horror.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c12">''Unsouled'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]] is the highest-ranking young [[Iron]] in the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c12" />}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Kelsa]] is a Wei Shi.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c22">''Underlord'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Kelsa]] is being hunted by a sacred beast.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c22" />}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Kelsa]] possesses [[White Fox]] [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c1">''Uncrowned'', chapter 1</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Kelsa]] has recently earned her eyes and possesses newborn spiritual sense.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Kelsa]] has [[Skyhunter Iron body]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Kelsa]]'s mother is a prisoner in [[Heaven's Glory]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28" />}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Trained by [[Orthos]], Kelsa stands out as a unique individual among her kin in the [[Sacred Valley]]. During a battle where she utilized the [[Fox Dream]] [[Ruler technique]], she fought while exhausted and aware that broken panes of [[Forged madra]] burn like live coals. After witnessing a second creature arrive in the sky, she was left covered in ash and dust from her exhaustion. Later, while wearing a huge backpack, she could be seen pacing next to the turtle.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] uses the [[Fox Dream]] [[Ruler technique]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c7">''Bloodline'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] is exhausted during the battle.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] knows that broken panes of [[Forged madra]] burn like live coals.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] has been trained by [[Orthos]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8">''Bloodline'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] is unique among her kin in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9">''Bloodline'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] is pacing next to the turtle.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15">''Bloodline'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] is wearing a huge backpack.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] is a character who witnessed the arrival of a second creature in the sky.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]] is exhausted and covered in ash and dust.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A tall and broad individual, Wei Shi Kelsa is a founding member of the [[Twin Star sect]]. During a period of intense training, Kelsa advanced to the [[Lowgold]] stage while [[Little Blue]] became increasingly addicted to overseeing the process. As a practitioner, Kelsa utilizes [[Foxfire]] in both hands and possesses a [[Foxfire]] tail. Following these developments, Kelsa expressed feelings of gratitude.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Wei Shi [[Kelsa]] is a tall and broad individual.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6">''Reaper'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Kelsa]] is a founding member of the [[Twin Star sect]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Kelsa]] is undergoing training.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7">''Reaper'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Little Blue]] has grown addicted to training [[Kelsa]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Kelsa]] advanced to [[Lowgold]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Kelsa]] uses [[Foxfire]] in both hands.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c19">''Reaper'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Kelsa]] has a [[Foxfire]] tail.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24">''Reaper'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Kelsa]] expressed gratitude.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24" />}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|As the youngest and strongest [[Lowgold]] of the [[Wei clan]], Kelsa practices the [[Path of the White Fox]] and is known for her bold, direct manner of speaking. While she has only ever served as a teacher to [[Lindon]], who worked on creating a [[construct]] for her, she is also a capable combatant. During a confrontation with [[Jai Long]], she utilized [[Striker]] and [[Foxtail]] [[Enforcer techniques]] alongside her [[Foxfire]]. Following a period where she and [[Jai Chen]] huddled in fear, Kelsa eventually appeared wearing a stony mask and answering questions with a hollow expression.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] is creating a [[construct]] for [[Kelsa]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4">''Dreadgod'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] trains the [[Path of the White Fox]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] is usually bold and direct in her speech.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Wei Shi [[Kelsa]] is the youngest and strongest [[Lowgold]] of the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] is a teacher who has only ever trained [[Lindon]] as a student.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] uses [[Foxfire]] to attack [[Jai Long]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] used the [[Foxtail]] [[Enforcer technique]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] uses a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] and [[Jai Chen]] huddled in fear.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18">''Dreadgod'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] is answering questions for a family with a hollow expression.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Kelsa]] is wearing a stony mask.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Wei Shi [[Kelsa]] is the sister of the [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Kelsa is the older sister of [[Lindon]] and a stable [[Truegold]] who follows the [[Path of the White Fox]]. Clad in plain gray [[sacred artist]]’s robes, she possesses a [[Skyhunter Iron body]] and utilizes a [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]] to fly. Her combat style relies on confusion, distraction, and erosion through the use of a [[void key]] and an invisible [[sword]] kept unseen by combining wind with [[White Fox]] [[madra]]. She further employs the [[Foxtail]] [[Enforcer technique]], uses [[Fox Dream]] to generate illusions, and carries a buckler shield that expands from her forearm bracer.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] is [[Lindon]]'s older sister.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] is a [[Truegold]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] follows the [[Path of the White Fox]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] is a [[Truegold]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] wears a plain gray [[sacred artist]]’s robe.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] has a [[void key]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] uses an invisible [[sword]] as a weapon.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] uses a combination of [[White Fox]] [[madra]] and wind to keep her [[sword]] unseen.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] uses the [[Foxtail]] [[Enforcer technique]] from the [[Path of the White Fox]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]]'s combat style focuses on confusion, distraction, and erosion.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] possesses a buckler shield that expands from a bracer on her forearm.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] uses [[Fox Dream]] to create illusions.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] possesses a [[Skyhunter Iron body]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] uses a [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]] to fly.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] is a stable [[Truegold]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c13">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] is [[Lindon]]'s sister.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c13" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|“Kelsa’s advancing to Iron,” he announced, then he ran back the other direction.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c7" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Behind her mother, Kelsa nodded. “Don't push yourself too hard. If they shame you too badly, I'll pay them back in the Iron trials.”<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Kelsa walked up behind him, her mouth open in horror. “Is that…”<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“Wei Shi Kelsa, sister of the Void, speaks with his voice.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|“I’m Lindon’s older sister,” Kelsa pointed out<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle characters]]
[[Category:Wei clan]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Sacred artists]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
